prediction.reasoning: Using historical volatility analysis for Goldman Sachs (GS), I estimated an annualized volatility of approximately 25%. For an 18-day forecast horizon, this translates to roughly 6% expected price movement range. The point estimate assumes a random walk with no drift, while confidence intervals are constructed using log-normal distribution assumptions typical for stock prices. The intervals widen with higher confidence levels to account for increased uncertainty over the 18-day period.
